seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with LangChain enables sophisticated email processing through chains for intent detection, draft generation, and approval workflows. However, the high-risk nature of email handling demands robust guardrails to prevent PII leaks, inappropriate tone, and inaccurate content that could damage relationships or expose sensitive data. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure your LangChain-powered assistant operates safely and reliably.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant typically starts with an intent detection chain that classifies incoming emails (reply, forward, draft, etc.) using a prompted LLM. This feeds into parallel or sequential chains for content analysis, attachment handling via tools, and draft generation with RAG for user context. An agent executor orchestrates tool calls for email sending approval, recipient validation, and final formatting before output.

LangChain's RouterChain or AgentExecutor manages the workflow, pulling from vector stores for email history and user preferences. Common patterns include conversational memory for context and custom tools for SMTP integration or calendar checks. The high-risk email domain requires verification at key choke points: input parsing, draft generation, and send approval.

Vex wraps critical `chain.invoke()` and `agent_executor.invoke()` calls to enforce PII filtering, tone analysis, and compliance checks before any external action.

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
risks.description: Agent fabricates email content or references non-existent conversations from faulty vector store retrieval
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies factual accuracy against email history and user context before draft generation

risks.name: Tool call loops in agent executors
risks.description: Agent repeatedly calls SMTP or calendar tools without progress, consuming resources and delaying response
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors tool call patterns and enforces max iterations with fallback responses

risks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
risks.description: Thread history exceeds token limits, causing truncated context and incoherent replies
risks.mitigation: Vex summarizes conversation history and enforces context window limits dynamically

risks.name: Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
risks.description: Draft generator and approval chain produce mismatched email formats, breaking SMTP compatibility
risks.mitigation: Vex standardizes output schema across all chain steps with structured verification

implementation.title: Intent Detection Chain
implementation.description: Classifies email intent using prompted LLM chain with conversation memory
implementation.code:
from langchain.chains import LLMChain
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate
from langchain.memory import ConversationBufferMemory

intent_prompt = PromptTemplate(
    input_variables=["email_content", "history"],
    template="""Classify this email intent: reply, forward, draft_new, archive, delete.
Email: {email_content}
History: {history}
Intent:"""
)

memory = ConversationBufferMemory(memory_key="history", return_messages=True)
intent_chain = LLMChain(
    llm=ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4"),
    prompt=intent_prompt,
    memory=memory,
    verbose=True
)

result = intent_chain.invoke({"email_content": "Please review Q3 report attached"})

implementation.title: Email Draft Generation Agent
implementation.description: Agent with tools for draft creation, attachment handling, and recipient validation
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, Tool
from langchain.tools import BaseTool

class EmailDraftTool(BaseTool):
    name = "generate_draft"
    description = "Generate professional email draft"
    
    def _run(self, intent: str, context: str) -> str:
        return f"Subject: Re: {intent}\n\n{context}"

agent_tools = [EmailDraftTool()]
agent = initialize_agent(
    tools=agent_tools,
    llm=ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4"),
    agent="zero-shot-react-description",
    verbose=True
)

draft = agent.run("Create reply draft for sales meeting follow-up")
